name: Region Select Intent
summary: A cross-cutting spatial-selection vocabulary (placement ratified #1463) — the declarative layer naming HOW a pointer-drawn region picks items: the shape it draws (rect | lasso | polygon | nearest), the hit-test mode that decides membership (intersect | contain | center), and the modifier that combines the hit with the existing selection (replace | add | toggle | subtract). Designed in full up front (completeness-early beats YAGNI for a standard's vocabulary, #1463) so the modifier/hit-test contract is proven against the whole shape set rather than retrofitted when a late shape appears. Composes the Selection Intent (it drives a multi-selection) — region-select owns the spatial GESTURE→region→hit-set; selection owns the resulting choice state. UX-only: the geometry engine (AABB / point-in-polygon / centroid) is an impl seam in the FUI realizations (marquee-select = the rect member), NOT part of this intent.

shape.description: The geometry the pointer draws to define the region — the declarative "what shape". `rect` is the drag-out axis-aligned band (the marquee, #1406); `lasso` is a freehand closed path (point-in-polygon over the traced vertices); `polygon` is a click-to-add vertex path (point-in-polygon over explicit vertices); `nearest` picks the single item whose centroid is closest to the pointer (a degenerate zero-area region). These four are the convergent set across design tools (Figma/Sketch marquee + lasso, Illustrator polygon, snapping 'nearest'), data-viz brushing, and OS file managers — designed in full so every realization declares its `shape` against a settled contract. Open-numbered per the variant contract (intents are open-design); authors may mint further region shapes.

mode.description: The hit-test rule that decides whether an item is in the region — the membership predicate, shared across all shapes. `intersect`: the item is selected if the region overlaps it at all (the permissive default, matching the drag-right marquee in most tools). `contain`: only fully-enclosed items count (the stricter drag-left / 'window' selection, mirroring CAD). `center`: an item counts if its centre point falls inside the region. Optional with a most-permissive default: absent → `intersect`. Grounded in the FUI marquee hit-test core (`MarqueeMode`).

modifier.description: How the freshly hit set combines with the existing selection when the region resolves — the set-algebra op, driven by the held keyboard modifier at gesture end. `replace`: the region's hits become the selection (the no-modifier default). `add` (Shift): union the hits in. `toggle` (Cmd/Ctrl): flip each hit's membership. `subtract` (Alt): remove the hits from the selection. Optional with default `replace`. Grounded in the FUI marquee `MarqueeModifier` vocab + `modifierFromEvent` mapping; the keyboard-equivalent obligation rides the composed Selection Intent.

description:
What This Is

A cross-cutting spatial-selection vocabulary (placement ratified in #1463): the declarative layer that names how a pointer-drawn region picks items. The platform ships raw Pointer Events but no region-selection primitive — every design tool, data-viz brush, and file manager hand-rolls a marquee. This intent owns the declaration (this surface is rect / lasso / polygon / nearest selectable); the geometry engine is a swappable impl in the realizations.

Designed in full up front (completeness-early)

The full shape set — rect · lasso · polygon · nearest — is named at mint, not grown one member at a time. Per the #1463 GO ruling, completeness-early beats YAGNI for a standard's vocabulary: settling the whole shape set lets the shared mode / modifier contract be proven against every member (rect AABB, lasso/polygon point-in-polygon, nearest centroid) rather than retrofitted when a late shape appears. #1406 marquee-select becomes the rect realization; the others follow from settled incumbent geometry.

Dimensions (UX-only)

  • shape: the region geometry — rect · lasso · polygon · nearest, the convergent core, open-numbered (authors extend).
  • mode: the hit-test membership rule — intersect · contain · center; absent → intersect (the most-permissive default).
  • modifier: the set-algebra op combining hits with the selection — replace · add · toggle · subtract; absent → replace.

Composes Selection — the region→choice seam

Region-select owns recognition of the spatial region and the hit-set it resolves to; the resulting choice state lives in the Selection Intent (single vs multi, urgency). A region drives a multi-selection — the two compose, never conflict: region-select is the spatial gesture → hit-set, selection is the persisted chosen set. The keyboard / single-pointer equivalent obligation (a region is never the only path to a multi-selection) rides the composed Selection contract.

UX-only → the geometry is an impl seam

The how — axis-aligned-band math for rect, point-in-polygon for lasso/polygon, centroid distance for nearest, plus the Douglas-Peucker simplify tolerance and polygon close-trigger — is impl, not part of this intent. It lives in the Frontier UI realizations: marquee-select (the rect member) generalizes its geometry core into a shared region-select module that the lasso / polygon / nearest members consume. gesture is the structural precedent — a recognizer-adjacent intent whose engine is a swappable impl seam, not a dimension.
